title: dacryodes dahomensis
intro: Dacryodes dahomensis, also known as African oil tree or African walnut, is a tropical plant native to West Africa, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The plant's seeds are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The seeds have been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in the management of diabetes and hypertension. Dacryodes dahomensis seeds can be prepared into herbal remedies like oil, powder, or decoctions, often used in folk medicine to address various health issues.
